framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Publish your own testing methodology in writing
paths.what_it_is: Write and maintain a public body of work — blog, book, or course — that lays out a distinct, opinionated approach to how testing should be done, not just notes on tools.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you've developed an original point of view on how testing should work, not just familiarity with a checklist someone else wrote.

paths.path: Build and open-source your own test automation tooling
paths.what_it_is: Design, build, and publicly release a reusable automation library, framework, or course repository — not just use existing tools like Selenium or Appium, but extend or rebuild parts of that ecosystem yourself.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves engineering craft beyond tool proficiency — you can build and give away infrastructure other testers actually adopt.

paths.path: Teach live on conference and community stages
paths.what_it_is: Repeatedly propose, prepare, and deliver talks or workshops at testing conferences and community events, building a track record of public teaching in front of practitioner audiences.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can explain your expertise clearly in front of a live, skeptical audience, not just execute it quietly on your own team.
